channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Journalist's Father Imprisoned Over Recruitment Scandal Linked to State House
item.description: Baba Kimani, father of journalist Kimani Mbugua, is in prison after being implicated in a recruitment scam linked to State House officials. According to TikToker Okumu, Baba Kimani was asked to mobili...

item.title: PPP's Bilawal Aims for Stronger Show in AJK Elections as Party Seeks Government Formation
item.description: PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari expressed hopes of forming a government in Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) during a public rally in Kotli, emphasizing the need for improved election results compared ...

item.title: Astronomers Confirm Atmosphere on Rocky Exoplanet in a Habitable Zone
item.description: Astronomers have confirmed that the rocky exoplanet LHS 1140 b possesses an atmosphere while orbiting within the habitable zone of its host star, making it one of the most promising Earth-like worlds ...

item.title: Wildfire Evacuees Share Harrowing Experiences in Northwestern Ontario
item.description: Over 350 people from Whitesand First Nation and neighboring communities have evacuated due to wildfires burning across northern Ontario. Retired firefighter Daniel Nodin warned residents of the danger...

item.title: Two BARMM Towns Experience Back-to-Back Grenade Attacks
item.description: A violent incident unfolded in two different towns under the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) as unidentified motorcycle riders hurled grenades at military checkpoints, injuring...

item.title: Top Cartoons of July 20, 2026: Political Satire and Commentary
item.description: The article features a curated selection of political cartoons published on July 20, 2026, highlighting key issues in Australian politics. The cartoons provide sharp commentary on government policies,...

item.title: Zungu calls for revisiting Safa constitution to prevent factional misuse
item.description: Sandile Zungu, a presidential candidate for the South African Football Association (Safa), has criticized the current use of the association's constitution as a tool for political advantage. The 2022 ...

item.title: Governor Otti commits to paying disengaged workers' entitlements
item.description: Governor Alex Otti of Abia State has assured workers laid off by previous administrations that their outstanding entitlements will be paid after verification. During a civic reception in Aba, Otti hig...

item.title: Trump's Airport Rebranding Faces Funding Crisis as County Officials Reject Payment
item.description: President Donald Trump's rebranding of Palm Beach International Airport to the 'President Donald J. Trump International Airport' has encountered significant obstacles. While the airport was officially...

item.title: Pete Buttigieg criticizes Stephen Miller's anti-leftist rhetoric
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's Transportation Secretary, Pete Buttigieg, criticized White House Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller's aggressive rhetoric against 'leftists' during an appearance on MS N...

item.title: Delhi High Court Supports Government's Action to Shift Fasting Activist Sonam Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital
item.description: The Delhi High Court has rejected a plea by Gitanjali Angmo, wife of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, seeking his transfer from Safdarjung Hospital to a private facility like Medanta. The court observ...

item.title: Koeberg nuclear plant confirms no public risk after routine safety checks
item.description: South Africa's Koeberg nuclear power station, the country's only nuclear facility, has confirmed there is no threat to the public following minor radioactive contamination incidents during scheduled m...

item.title: Political Tensions in Ol Kalou: Call for Peaceful Democratic Competition
item.description: Dennis Itumbi, Digital Strategist and Head of Creative Economy in the Office of the President, has raised alarms over escalating political tensions in Mt Kenya. He revealed that he had to evacuate 20 ...

item.title: Tanzanian Youth Achieve Historic Success in Gothia Cup Under-17 Tournament
item.description: A Tanzanian player has made history by securing the Gothia Cup under-17 title in Sweden, marking a significant milestone for Tanzanian football. The achievement highlights the growing talent and poten...

item.title: New judicial oversight body for IDAC establishes complaints procedures
item.description: The Office of the IDAC Oversight Judge, established under Section 22A of the National Prosecuting Authority Amendment Act, has outlined its role in providing independent judicial oversight of the Inve...

item.title: Vice President Shettima Attends ECOWAS Summit To Boost Regional Cooperation In West Africa
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima has left Abuja for Freetown, Sierra Leone, to represent President Bola Tinubu at the 69th Ordinary Session of the ECOWAS Authority of Heads of State and Government. The ...

item.title: Red Blood Cell Parameters Show Potential in Differentiating Parkinsonian Syndromes
item.description: A study published in Scientific Reports explores the use of red blood cell-derived parameters, particularly red cell distribution width-coefficient of variation (RDW-CV), as a potential tool for diffe...

item.title: Blue Angels Pilot Admits Mistake in Low-Altitude Flyover Over Florida Beach Amid Political Reactions
item.description: A U.S. Navy Blue Angels pilot involved in a controversial low-altitude flyover over Pensacola, Florida, has reportedly admitted the maneuver was a mistake rather than a political statement. The incide...

item.title: Canadian FM's Strategic Visit to Pakistan Focuses on Trade and Bilateral Ties
item.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand is set to visit Pakistan this week, marking the first such visit in 20 years. The announcement by Global Affairs Canada highlights efforts to strengthen strategic...

item.title: Canadian Tourism Shifts Amid U.S. Trade Tensions
item.description: Canadians are increasingly avoiding travel to the United States due to political tensions and trade disputes, marking a significant shift in cross-border tourism patterns. This boycott, driven by oppo...
